name: Valion Bio, Inc.
business: Tivic Health Systems, Inc. (VBIO) is a late-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing TLR5 agonist Entolimod for Acute Radiation Syndrome and oncology supportive care indications such as neutropenia, thrombocytopenia, and mucositis. The company also operates a contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) subsidiary, Velocity Bioworks, to support its own manufacturing and serve third-party clients. Tivic derives no current product revenue as its lead programs are investigational; it exited its consumer healthtech business in 2025.
sellsTo: U.S. government agencies (BARDA, DoD/DTRA) for Strategic National Stockpile procurement
Oncology patients and healthcare providers (via future commercial sales for neutropenia and other indications)
namedSuppliers: Scorpius Holdings, Inc. (acquired assets to form Velocity Bioworks)

exposures.sentence: Tivic Health Systems, Inc. is a late-stage biopharmaceutical company whose lead program is Entolimod.
At the same time, Tivic exited the consumer healthtech business and suspended its prescription bioelectronic product development efforts in order to focus resources fully on Entolimod development.

Velocity Bioworks, a wholly owned subsidiary, is intended to function as a full-service contract development and manufacturing organization (CDMO) focused on rapidly advancing biologic programs to the clinic.

catalysts.event: Exclusive worldwide license to Entolimod for ARS from Statera Biopharma
catalysts.sentence: February 2025: Tivic acquired worldwide exclusive license rights to the Toll-like Receptor f (TLR5) agonist, Entolimod, for acute radiation syndrome from Statera Biopharma, Inc.

catalysts.event: Appointment of Michael K. Handley as President of Biopharma and COO
catalysts.sentence: Q1 2025: Tivic began building its Biopharma leadership with the hiring of industry veteran, Michael K. Handley, to lead the business unit as President of Biopharma and serve as the company's Chief Operating Officer.

catalysts.event: Expansion of license to include neutropenia rights and Entolasta
catalysts.sentence: Q2 2025: Tivic expanded its license agreement with Statera to include neutropenia rights and rights to Entolimod's second generation molecule, Entolasta.

catalysts.event: Cell line verification at Scorpius site
catalysts.sentence: Q3 2025: Verification of the Entolimod cell line at the Scorpius Biomanufacturing site, from which assets were later acquired to form Velocity Bioworks.

catalysts.event: Acquisition of Scorpius Biomanufacturing assets to form Velocity Bioworks CDMO
catalysts.sentence: December 2025: Acquisition of the manufacturing and development assets of Scorpius Biomanufacturing, establishing Velocity Bioworks as a wholly owned CDMO subsidiary.

catalysts.event: Ceased sales of ClearUP device and wound down consumer business
catalysts.sentence: December 2025: The Company ceased sales of its ClearUP sinus device and related operations as of year-end 2025.

catalysts.event: Manufacturing scale-up demonstration at 50-liter fermentation volume
catalysts.sentence: January 2026: Velocity Bioworks demonstrated a 200-fold (200x) manufacturing scale-up for Entolimod at 50-liter fermentation volume, meeting all drug release specifications for purity and potency.

catalysts.event: Active engagement with BARDA, DoW/DTRA, FDA, NIH, NIAID via TechWatch process
catalysts.sentence: January March 2026: Active engagement with BARDA, DoW/DTRA, FDA, NIH, and NIAID via TechWatch process; a follow-up meeting took place on March 10, 2026, to advance discussions on federal funding of future testing and potential path to a Strategic National Stockpile purchase.

catalysts.event: Appointment of Michael K. Handley as CEO
catalysts.sentence: On March 3, 2026, we appointed Michael K. Handley as Chief Executive Officer of the Company and a member of the board of directors.

catalysts.event: Received Nasdaq non-compliance notice for bid price below $1.00
catalysts.sentence: On March 19, 2026, the Company received a notification letter from the Nasdaq Listing Qualifications Department ... notifying the Company that, because the closing bid price for the Company's common stock was below $1.00 per share for at least 30 consecutive business days, the Company is not currently in compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.

catalysts.event: Filed IND and initiated Phase 2 preparation for neutropenia (exercise of option milestone)
catalysts.sentence: On June 18, 2025, the Company entered into an Amended and Restated Exclusive License Agreement ... which amended certain terms ...

tamClaims.claim: Global Acute Radiation Syndrome market valued at USD 5.47 Bn in 2025, expected to reach USD 7.80 Bn by 2032 (5.2% CAGR).
tamClaims.sentence: According to CoherntAI, 'the global Acute Radiation Syndrome Market is estimated to be valued at USD 5.47 Bn in 2025 and is expected to reach USD 7.80 Bn by 2032, growing at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.2% from 2025 to 2032.'

tamClaims.claim: Global G-CSF market valued at ~$8.3B in 2022-23 (~$4.5B U.S.).
tamClaims.sentence: As a result, the global G-CSF market was valued at ~$8.3B in 2022 23 (~$4.5B U.S.).

tamClaims.claim: Febrile neutropenia drives ~60,000-80,000 U.S. hospitalizations annually at $20,000-$30,000 each, economic burden exceeding $3 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: It drives ~60,000 80,000 U.S. hospitalizations annually at $20,000 $30,000 each, an economic burden exceeding $3 billion.

tamClaims.claim: Annual U.S. costs of mucositis-related complications exceed $2 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Annual U.S. costs of mucositis-related complications exceed $2 billion.
